summary: 14 acceptance tests written in internal/acceptance/swarmforge_cli_test.go — all RED. Stub packages created with interfaces and unimplemented functions. The Coder must implement each package to make tests pass.
instructions:
14 acceptance tests are RED in internal/acceptance/swarmforge_cli_test.go. Stub packages exist with types/interfaces defined but functions returning 'not implemented' errors or empty strings. Follow TDD: for EACH package, write unit tests first, then implement. Reference swarmforge.sh for exact behavior.

Packages to implement (in suggested order):
1. internal/preflight — Check() iterates deps, calls lookPath, returns first error mentioning the dep name
2. internal/banner — Print() writes SwarmForge ASCII banner with motto
3. internal/prompt/roles.go — Fill in ArchitectInstructions, CoderInstructions, E2EInterpreterInstructions constants (copy from swarmforge.sh)
4. internal/prompt — Build() generates full agent prompt matching swarmforge.sh build_agent_prompt format
5. internal/setup — EnsureDirs() creates features/logs/agent_context; WriteHelperScripts() writes notify-agent.sh and swarm-log.sh
6. internal/swarmlog — Logger.Write() formats '[TIMESTAMP] [role] message' and writes to all writers
7. internal/tmux — CreateSession, SplitGrid, SetPaneTitles, KillSession, LaunchAgent, SendKeys — each calls cmd.Run with correct tmux args (see swarmforge.sh lines 145-189)
8. internal/notify — Notify() logs '[pane N] message' via logger then calls tmux send-keys
9. internal/cli — Dispatch() routes 'start'/'notify'/'log' to handlers, returns usage error for empty args
10. internal/start — Run() orchestrates: check HasSession, kill if exists, create session, split grid, set titles, launch agents, init metrics pane

Key details from swarmforge.sh:
- tmux new-session -d -s SESSION -n WINDOW
- split-window -t SESSION:WINDOW.0 -h -p 50, then -t .0 -v -p 50, then -t .2 -v -p 50
- select-pane -t SESSION:WINDOW.N -T 'Title'
- pane-border-status top, pane-border-format ' #{pane_title} '
- claude --append-system-prompt-file PROMPTFILE --permission-mode acceptEdits -n 'SwarmForge NAME'
- Pane layout: 0=Architect, 1=E2E Interpreter, 2=Coder, 3=Metrics

The acceptance tests use RecordingCommander and FakeFS stubs (defined in the test file) — your production code must accept the tmux.Commander and setup.FS interfaces.

Run: go test ./internal/acceptance/ -run TestCLI_ -v to verify progress. All 14 must pass.
